evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix with row-normalised percentages, diagonal annotated with % and raw count, off-diagonal coloured by Blues sequential cmap — exactly matches brief.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=none set; Arial first in font stack (not DejaVu default); pdffonts confirms Arial TrueType fully embedded; cell text 6.5–7.5pt, axis labels 8–9pt, title 11pt.
evidence.palette: Blues sequential cmap for off-diagonal is colorblind-safe and perceptually uniform; diagonal uses a custom dark-blue gradient; no green/red directional colours anywhere.
evidence.layout_density: 7×6 in figure for 100 cells with tight_layout(); off-diagonal annotation at 6.5pt is borderline small for print; no overlapping labels but whitespace around the matrix could be tighter.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption gives specific numerical claim (C8 at 92%) and explains colour encoding, but there is no second colorbar for the diagonal scale — a reviewer would ask how to read diagonal colour intensity quantitatively.
evidence.vector_fidelity: Text is fully vector (Arial TrueType embedded, pdf.fonttype=42); however pdfinfo shows two raster XObject Images (508×507 and 21×431 px) — the imshow heatmap cells and colorbar gradient are rasterised, not vector paths.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (40 KB), figure.png (152 KB), figure.svg (53 KB), caption.tex (511 B), gen_figure.py (5 KB) all present and non-trivial; brief required pdf+png+caption+script — all satisfied.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states row-normalised rates, identifies best-performing class with exact value (C8 at 92%), explains dual colour encoding for diagonal vs off-diagonal, and reports N=100 per class.

overall_strengths: Proper font embedding (pdf.fonttype=42, Arial TrueType) with svg.fonttype=none ensures editable, selectable text across all output formats.
Colorblind-safe Blues palette with no directional colour misuse, and a well-structured caption that includes a specific numerical finding and explains the colour encoding.
overall_weaknesses: The heatmap cells and colorbar are rasterised XObject images inside the PDF — the colour grid is not true vector paths, limiting lossless rescaling.
No colorbar for the diagonal colour scale; readers cannot quantitatively interpret diagonal cell shading without a legend, which a reviewer would flag.
